title: Respect Autofill, Placeholder, and Motion Preferences
summary: Build and verify css form autofill motion in completed-field readability and motion preference incident through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css form autofill motion from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css form autofill motion without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Completed-field readability and motion preference incident case 1: autofill colors can be overridden without checking readability or user expectations.

Completed-field readability and motion preference incident case 1: inspect browser states, preserve readable data and labels, keep overrides bounded, and remove nonessential transitions under reduced motion.
Completed-field readability and motion preference incident case 1: make autofilled text transparent so browser highlight colors fit the theme.

steps.title: 05 · Read the completed-field readability and motion preference incident case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Which claim preserves css form autofill motion when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: completed-field readability and motion preference incident.
steps.why: Respect Autofill, Placeholder, and Motion Preferences makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 5 in completed-field readability and motion preference incident.

content.text: Browsers expose autofill, placeholder, search decorations, and other platform-specific states differently. Styling must keep stored data readable and recognizable rather than fighting the browser. Placeholder remains supporting example text, not a label. Motion around focus, error, and success should be optional and disabled or reduced under prefers-reduced-motion. Apply it to completed-field readability and motion preference incident.
